nodes.name: 14th Lowland
nodes.excerpt: The 14th Lowland is an infantry regiment known for labor and construction duties.
nodes.notes: The 14th Lowland is a provincial infantry regiment where Dellan Moor served for four years. The regiment was primarily used for labor and construction tasks. Sable Voss was a supply administrator in this regiment. Dellan deserted from this regiment fourteen months ago.

nodes.name: Admiralty
nodes.excerpt: The organization Maren Ashveil worked for as a surveyor.
nodes.notes:
The Admiralty is the organization Maren Ashveil worked for as a licensed surveyor for a decade before resigning under undisclosed circumstances. An incident during a mapping expedition led to her voluntary withdrawal.

The Cartographic Division is a branch of the Admiralty responsible for maintaining official navigational charts, survey records, and coastal data. It operates out of the Admiralty's main building in Tiveck.

The Cartographic Division is a branch of the Admiralty responsible for maintaining official navigational charts and survey records. It operates out of the Admiralty's main building in Tiveck, with its archive in a separate vault.

nodes.name: Akaashi
nodes.excerpt: Akaashi is a customer at the coffeeshop who is a photography student.
nodes.notes:
Akaashi is a quiet and reserved photography student who frequents the coffeeshop where Bokuto works. He is described as beautiful and carries a camera with him, indicating his passion for photography. Akaashi is initially indifferent to Bokuto's flirtations but eventually offers him a modeling job for a photography project. Over time, Akaashi and Bokuto develop a deeper connection, with Akaashi showing a dry sense of humor and a gentle demeanor. He is focused on his work and values professionalism, as seen when he insists on waiting until the project is over before pursuing a personal relationship with Bokuto.

Akaashi Keiji is introduced as Kageyama's father, a supportive and caring parent who shares his son's interest in volleyball. He is depicted as a handsome and composed individual, catching Bokuto's attention and sparking a romantic interest. Akaashi's interactions with Bokuto are polite and respectful, and he is shown to be a skilled volleyball player himself, participating in games with the children. His character adds depth to the narrative, bridging the gap between the children's world and the adult responsibilities of parenthood.

His character adds depth to the narrative, bridging the gap between the children's world and the adult responsibilities of parenthood.

nodes.name: Asas São Paulo
nodes.excerpt: Asas São Paulo is a professional team in the Brazil Super League.

nodes.name: Bokuto
nodes.excerpt: Bokuto is a barista at a coffeeshop who becomes infatuated with a customer named Akaashi.
nodes.notes:
Bokuto is a lively and charismatic barista working at a coffeeshop called Nekoma's. He is immediately taken by Akaashi, a customer who visits the shop, and makes a bold attempt to flirt with him by writing his phone number on a cup. Despite Akaashi's initial lack of response, Bokuto remains hopeful and continues to be intrigued by Akaashi's quiet demeanor and passion for photography. Throughout the story, Bokuto's interactions with Akaashi evolve from awkward flirtations to a genuine connection, as they work together on Akaashi's photography project. Bokuto is characterized by his enthusiasm, humor, and a tendency to act impulsively, especially when it comes to matters of the heart.

Bokuto is a professional volleyball player who is deeply involved in his son Shouyou's life. He is depicted as a caring and attentive father, willing to go the extra mile to ensure his son's happiness and well-being. Bokuto is also shown to have a humorous and light-hearted personality, often engaging in playful banter with his son. His interactions with Kageyama's father, Akaashi Keiji, reveal a budding interest and attraction, adding a layer of complexity to his character. Bokuto's passion for volleyball is evident, and he takes pride in sharing this passion with the younger generation, including Kageyama Tobio.

nodes.name: Boris Hulwich
nodes.excerpt: Boris Hulwich is a weathered wanderer shaped by loss and exile.
nodes.notes: Boris Hulwich, once the son of a minor noble house on the fringes of Veldmoor, lost his family during a brutal border conflict that erased his lineage from official record. Stripped of title and inheritance, he became a wanderer—part mercenary, part reluctant guardian—moving through the marshlands and ruined keeps of the region. Known for his imposing build, ash-gray eyes, and calm voice, Boris carries himself with restraint, hiding a constant tension between vengeance and hope. His primary motive is survival with purpose, often drawn into conflicts due to a moral code he cannot escape. His closest bond is with Sable Voss, characterized by mutual reliance and a complex dynamic.
nodes.filePreview:
He is known for his imposing build, ash-gray eyes, and a voice that rarely rises above a measured calm. Boris carries himself with restraint, but beneath that control lies a constant tension—an internal battle between vengeance and the faint hope of rebuilding something meaningful. He is pragmatic, often blunt, but guided by a deeply buried sense of loyalty that he refuses to acknowledge openly.

Boris’s primary motive is survival, but not in the simple sense—he seeks purpose in a world that has already taken everything from him. Though he claims detachment, he is drawn repeatedly into confl…

nodes.name: The Cartographic Division
nodes.excerpt: The Cartographic Division is the branch of the Admiralty responsible for maintaining all official navigational charts, survey records, and coastal data for the region.
nodes.notes: The Cartographic Division operates out of the upper two floors of the Admiralty's main building in Tiveck, with its archive in a separate, humidity-controlled vault in the basement. It employs approximately forty-five people, including surveyors, chart drafters, archivists, and administrative staff. The Division is headed by Chief Surveyor Vane, who has largely delegated operational responsibility to his deputy, Harwick Ossel. The Division's records are authoritative and play a crucial role in resolving disputes about coastal rights, territorial claims, or navigational precedent. Access to the archive is restricted and unauthorized access is treated as a criminal matter. The Division has a formally cooperative but informally complex relationship with the Deepwater Guild, with occasional disputes over survey data.

nodes.name: Chief Surveyor Vane
nodes.excerpt: Chief Surveyor Vane is an elderly man who has largely delegated operational responsibility to his deputy, Harwick Ossel.
nodes.notes: Chief Surveyor Vane is the nominal head of the Cartographic Division. He is described as elderly and visits the office three mornings a week, leaving before midday. Despite his position, he has delegated most of the operational responsibilities to his deputy, Harwick Ossel. Vane's role is crucial in authorizing access to the Division's archives, which are considered authoritative in resolving disputes related to coastal rights and navigational precedents.

nodes.name: Coach Ukai
nodes.excerpt: Coach Ukai was wrapping himself up in warm scarves.

nodes.name: Daichi
nodes.excerpt: Daichi just looked at him knowingly.
nodes.notes: Daichi is a character who shares a knowing camaraderie with Sugawara. He is a team member who understands the dynamics between Hinata and Kageyama and often steps in to mediate when tensions rise. Daichi's presence is calming, and he is seen as a leader who helps maintain harmony within the team.

nodes.name: Deepwater Guild
nodes.excerpt: A loose network of small-cargo operators and traders at the eastern quay.
nodes.notes:
The Deepwater Guild is an informal network of small-cargo operators, independent vessel owners, and harbor-adjacent traders working the eastern quay. It operates in the gray area between legality and prohibition, with no formal leadership or membership list. Fen Caldra is a central figure due to his long-standing presence and trust among members. The Guild uses the Salt Warren for storage, with Fen holding the only key. Orris Vane, the harbor master, tolerates their operations to avoid drawing attention. The Guild reflects Tiveck's blend of formal and informal economies, with most members having deep roots in the city.

The Deepwater Guild has a formally cooperative but informally complex relationship with the Cartographic Division, often challenging the Division's survey data when it affects the Guild's operational reach.

The relationship between the Cartographic Division and the Deepwater Guild is formally cooperative but informally complex. The Guild has challenged the Division's survey data in situations where official charts would have reduced the Guild's operational reach, usually resolved quietly.
nodes.filePreview:
What it is: Not a formal guild in any legal or institutional sense — there is no charter, no dues, no membership list. The Deepwater Guild is the name that has attached itself, over several decades, to the loose network of small-cargo operators, independent vessel owners, and harbor-adjacent traders who work the eastern quay. The name is used half-seriously, a bit of self-aware humor: they are the people the official harbor economy doesn't quite account for.

Who belongs: Approximately a dozen regulars, another two dozen people who associate loosely. Common thread: they all operate in the sp…

nodes.name: Dellan Moor
nodes.excerpt: Dellan Moor is a former soldier living in hiding after deserting his regiment.
nodes.notes:
Background: Dellan is 29. Was a soldier — four years in a provincial infantry regiment, the 14th Lowland, stationed at a series of postings that moved him steadily away from anything he'd wanted when he enlisted. He deserted fourteen months ago, during a reassignment that would have sent him to a punitive posting in the eastern districts. He did not desert alone.

Current situation: He is living with Sable Voss in a disused highwayman's waystation on the moorland north of the main coastal road, roughly two days' walk from the nearest town of any size. The station has a roof that is mostly intact, a fireplace, and a well that still works. They have been here for eight months. Before that, they moved every few weeks. They have stopped moving because the waystation is good enough and because neither of them has a better plan.

How he lives: They grow what they can in the kitchen garden, supplemented by hunting and occasional careful trips to rural markets where nobody knows regimental deserters from ordinary travelers. He is competent at most practical things — carpentry, basic medicine, animal husbandry — because the 14th Lowland was primarily used for labor and construction in the years he served. He knows how to build things that last. He has been slowly repairing the waystation's outbuildings.

Relationship with Sable: They have known each other since the regiment. She was a supply administrator; they were not close until the decision to desert, which they made together over a period of about two weeks of careful, exhausted conversation. He is not certain whether what they have now is a permanent thing or a situation that made sense in extremity and has simply continued. He thinks about this more than he discusses it. He trusts her completely, which is a shorter list than he would have guessed.

Inner state: Less frightened than he was in the first months. More tired. Not unhappy, exactly — there is something clarifying about a day that has concrete physical tasks and no officers in it. But he is aware that the situation requires a longer-term solution and that neither of them has one yet. He does not let himself think too specifically about what happens if they're found.

Physical details: Lean, angular, darker complexion from years of outdoor postings. Keeps his hair short out of habit. Has a regimental tattoo on his left shoulder that he has not yet decided to remove.

nodes.name: Dovan Strake
nodes.excerpt: An individual with a complex relationship with Rella Corse.
nodes.notes:
Dovan Strake is known to have a long-running argument with Rella Corse, which cannot be fully characterized as either antagonism or friendship. The nature of their introduction is unknown.

Dovan Strake, when asked about the Inkhand, became less jovial and changed the subject, indicating discomfort or secrecy.

Dovan Strake is a founding member of the Tide Compact, handling buyers and laundering proceeds from document theft. He is affable, loud, and well-liked, frequenting the Wrecked Compass three nights a week.

Rella Corse met Dovan Strake during her freelance work period. They have a long-running argument that cannot be fully characterized as either antagonism or friendship.

Dovan Strake is a founding member of the Tide Compact, handling buyers and laundering proceeds from document theft. He operates out of a chandlery in Salt Warren and is well-liked, frequenting the Wrecked Compass.

Dovan Strake is a founding member of the Tide Compact, responsible for managing buyers. Known for his affable and loud personality, he defies expectations as a document theft launderer. Dovan operates out of a chandlery in Salt Warren and is a regular at The Wrecked Compass, where he is well-liked. His role in the Compact involves laundering proceeds and maintaining relationships with buyers, contributing to the group's success.

nodes.name: Fen Caldra
nodes.excerpt: A careful and discreet operator of a private transport service.
nodes.notes:
Background: Fen is in his mid-40s. Born in Tiveck, grew up on the docks, has never lived more than two miles from the waterfront. Spent his twenties working legitimate cargo vessels. Spent his thirties expanding his understanding of what "cargo" can mean. He now operates a small private transport service — two vessels, a rotating crew of four he trusts, and a reputation in certain circles for reliability and discretion. He is not violent. He is careful, which is a more sustainable quality.

Current operations: He runs cargo through the Salt Warren, a network of tidal caves and excavated chambers beneath the eastern cliff face of Tiveck. The Warren has been used for gray-market storage for generations — Fen didn't build it, he just maintains the right relationships to use it. He stores things there for people who don't want certain items in official warehouses. He asks what things are only when he needs to know for handling purposes.

The Sunken Ledger: Fen knows what it is. He found it six years ago in a sealed crate he was clearing from a Warren chamber the previous operator had abandoned. He read enough to understand its value — and its danger. He has not sold it, shown it to anyone, or destroyed it. It is still in the Warren, in a location only he knows. He has thought about what to do with it for six years and has not yet decided.

Relationship with Orrus Vane: Complex. Fen has leverage over Orrus in the form of the Warden's Seal — a forged customs document that Fen commissioned and then used to implicate Orrus in a cargo irregularity years ago. Orrus knows Fen has it. This is not a friendship. It is a functional arrangement that has kept both of them out of serious trouble for a long time. Fen would prefer not to use it. He keeps it because having it is useful.

Relationship with Maren Ashveil: He finds her interesting, which is unusual. Most of his clients are easy to read. Maren is harder. He's transported her twice without issue and done two small errands for her. He has not told her about the Ledger. He is fairly confident she is looking for it. He has not decided what to do about this.

Personality: Unhurried. Speaks in short sentences and waits to see how people respond to silence before continuing. Generous with small things — he'll buy a round, help carry something, remember a name. Careful with large ones. Doesn't make promises he hasn't already planned to keep.

Physical details: Stocky, weathered, gray coming into dark hair. Missing the top joint of the index finger on his right hand — lost it to a winch, years ago. Usually has a cup of something warm nearby that he forgets to drink.

nodes.name: Harwick Ossel
nodes.excerpt: Harwick Ossel is the deputy to Chief Surveyor Vane, handling operational responsibilities.
nodes.notes:
Harwick Ossel serves as the deputy to Chief Surveyor Vane in the Cartographic Division. He has taken on the operational responsibilities of the Division, indicating a significant level of trust and authority. Ossel's role involves managing the day-to-day functions of the Division, including overseeing the staff and ensuring the accuracy and integrity of the Division's records.

Harwick Ossel is a Senior Surveyor of the Admiralty and currently serves as the deputy head of the Cartographic Division. He is fifty-three, heavyset, with a deliberate manner that reads as gravitas to those who admire him and as obstruction to those who have worked against him. Ossel came up through the navigational corps the conventional way — thirty years of service, careful alliances, and a talent for being indispensable to whoever was making decisions at any given time. His skill is organizational, knowing where things are filed, who controls access to what, and which requests can be delayed indefinitely without formal objection. A complaint filed against him by Rella Corse alleged that he had been supplying altered survey data to a private concern, but the investigation found no corroborating evidence. Ossel lives in the upper district of Tiveck in rented accommodation considerably nicer than a deputy surveyor's salary would normally support. He is a member of two Admiralty social committees and is regarded as a capable administrator. There is a standing rumor — unverified — that he has a private arrangement with someone in the Deepwater Guild, which would constitute a significant breach of the Compact of Survey Rights.

nodes.name: The Inkhand
nodes.excerpt: A mysterious broker associated with the Tide Compact, known for using distinctive reddish ink.
nodes.notes:
The Inkhand is a name associated with the Tide Compact as its third founding member, whose identity remains unknown. They communicate exclusively through written notes in a distinctive reddish ink, described as rust-colored or dried-blood dark, and marked with a hand symbol. The Inkhand is believed to have access to Admiralty survey data and internal records from the Deepwater Guild, suggesting they might be an archivist or scribe within these institutions. The Inkhand's existence is shrouded in mystery, with speculation about their true identity or even their existence as a single person.

The Inkhand is a founding member of the Tide Compact. The role within the Compact is unclear, with some suggesting a connection to the Deepwater Guild, possibly as a scribe or archivist.

The Inkhand is the third founding member of the Tide Compact. They communicate exclusively through written notes using a distinctive reddish ink, described as rust-colored or dried-blood dark, and marked with a hand symbol. The Inkhand is believed to have access to Admiralty survey data and internal records from the Deepwater Guild, suggesting they might be an archivist or scribe. The Inkhand's identity remains unknown, and there is speculation they could be more than one person or a fictional entity. Rella Corse describes the arrangement with the Inkhand as functional, while Dovan Strake avoids discussing them. Maren Ashveil's name has been mentioned in connection with the Inkhand, but this is unverified.

The Inkhand is a founding member of the Tide Compact. Their role is unclear, with speculation about connections to the Deepwater Guild, possibly as a scribe or archivist.

The Inkhand is a name that appears in accounts of the Tide Compact as its third founding member, a broker or intermediary whose actual identity has never been established. The Inkhand communicates exclusively through written notes, using a distinctive reddish ink that some sources describe as rust-colored and others as dried-blood dark. The notes are always unsigned except for a small pressed mark: a hand, palm-forward, rendered in the same ink. Recipients describe the handwriting as precise and unhurried, the kind produced by someone with formal scribal training. The Inkhand is believed to have access to documents that would not normally be available outside of institutional archives — specifically, Admiralty survey data and, in at least one instance, an internal record from the Deepwater Guild that predated its official circulation by several days. This has led to speculation that the Inkhand is an archivist or scribe employed by one of these institutions, using their position to supply the Compact with information it can sell or use for leverage.

The Inkhand is an enigmatic figure within the Tide Compact, known for using distinctive reddish ink. Their true identity remains unconfirmed, leading to various theories about their role. Some suggest the Inkhand is the Compact's link to the Deepwater Guild, possibly a scribe or archivist with access to sensitive records. Others believe the Inkhand is a fictional creation to enhance the Compact's perceived influence. Despite the ambiguity, the Inkhand's presence adds an air of mystery and potential reach to the organization.

nodes.name: Iwaizumi
nodes.excerpt: Iwaizumi scowled at him.

nodes.name: Japan Men's National Volleyball Team
nodes.excerpt: Japan Men's National Volleyball Team participated in the 2021 Olympics.

nodes.name: Kageyama
nodes.excerpt: Kageyama ate politely and quietly.
nodes.notes:
Kageyama is a character who maintains a polite demeanor while eating but is quick to scold Hinata for his antics. He has a complex relationship with Hinata, characterized by both frustration and care. Kageyama is often seen taking care of Hinata, such as wiping his face or giving him his jacket when he's cold. Their relationship is central to the narrative, showcasing a blend of rivalry and affection.

Kageyama is portrayed as a serious and intense young boy with a passion for volleyball. He is a setter and aspires to be the best, showing a competitive spirit even at a young age. His relationship with Shouyou is complex; while he often comes across as grumpy and critical, there is an underlying respect and potential for friendship. Kageyama's interactions with Bokuto reveal his admiration for the sport and his desire to learn from experienced players. His father, Akaashi Keiji, plays a significant role in his life, supporting his volleyball interests.

nodes.name: Karasuno High
nodes.excerpt: Karasuno High is the school where Hinata and his teammates play volleyball.
nodes.notes: Karasuno High is a prominent setting in the Haikyū!! series, known for its Boys' Volleyball Team. The school was once famous for its volleyball team, particularly during the era of the 'Small Giant.' However, by the time Hinata joins, the team is struggling to regain its former glory. Under the guidance of Coach Ukai and the leadership of the third-year students, Karasuno aims to return to the national stage. The team is characterized by its determination and the unique quick attack combination of Hinata and Kageyama. Karasuno's rivalry with Nekoma High is a central theme, as both schools strive to meet each other at the national level.

nodes.name: Kenma
nodes.excerpt: Kenma is Kuroo's boyfriend, mentioned in the context of their relationship.

nodes.name: Kuroo
nodes.excerpt: Kuroo is Bokuto's coworker and friend at the coffeeshop.
nodes.notes: Kuroo works alongside Bokuto at the coffeeshop and is one of his close friends. He is characterized by his playful and teasing nature, often making jokes at Bokuto's expense, especially regarding his crush on Akaashi. Kuroo is supportive, though, and encourages Bokuto to pursue his interest in Akaashi. He is in a relationship with Kenma, and their dynamic is a source of amusement and warmth in the story.

nodes.name: Lev
nodes.excerpt: Lev is a coworker of Bokuto and Kuroo at the coffeeshop.
nodes.notes: Lev is another barista at the coffeeshop where Bokuto and Kuroo work. He is depicted as tall and somewhat laid-back, often seen lounging behind the cash register. Lev enjoys teasing Bokuto about his crush on Akaashi but is ultimately supportive of his friend. He has a cheerful disposition and is known for his loud reactions, particularly when startled or amused.

nodes.name: Maren Ashveil
nodes.excerpt: A client of Fen Caldra who is difficult to read and possibly seeking the Sunken Ledger.
nodes.notes:
Background: Maren is in her late 30s. Worked for a decade as a licensed surveyor for the Admiralty before resigning under circumstances she doesn't discuss in detail. The official record says "voluntary withdrawal." People who were there say something on a mapping expedition went wrong and Maren came back alone. She hasn't filed an Admiralty survey since. She does freelance work now — private clients, academic commissions, the occasional job that pays well and asks few questions.

Current situation: She arrived in Tiveck three weeks ago, ostensibly to document the harbor's shifting sandbar patterns for a merchant consortium. The sandbar work is real. It is not the reason she came. She came because she found a partial reference in an old Admiralty archive to something called the Sunken Ledger — a manifest of controlled cargo movements through Tiveck's gray market over a thirty-year period. The Ledger would implicate people who are still alive and still powerful. She hasn't told anyone why she's actually here.

How she got to Tiveck: She hired Fen Caldra for passage on a small coastal vessel. She told him she was a surveyor. He didn't ask follow-up questions, which she appreciated. She has used him twice since for discreet errands around the harbor.

Relationship with Orrus Vane: She introduced herself to Orrus professionally, as a surveyor seeking access to historical harbor records. He was cooperative — almost too cooperative, she thought. He gave her access to thirty years of tide logs and berthing records without much friction. She has since started to wonder whether he wants her to find something, or whether he's watching her to find out what she knows.

What she's found so far: The berthing records contain a recurring vessel identifier — a registration mark that appears in cargo manifests but not in any official vessel registry. It appears sixteen times over twenty-two years. The last entry is dated seven years ago. She thinks the Sunken Ledger is a physical document, not just a metaphor, and she thinks it's somewhere in Tiveck. She thinks Fen Caldra knows more about the Salt Warren than he's admitted.

Personality: Quiet in groups, precise in conversation. Doesn't volunteer information. Takes notes constantly and keeps them in a cipher of her own invention that looks like surveyor's shorthand from a distance. She has a habit of arriving early to places and watching who comes in after her. She is not paranoid. She is experienced.

Physical details: Mid-height, angular face, ink-stained fingers. Carries a leather map case that rarely leaves her shoulder. Dresses in practical layers. The scar on her left forearm is from the expedition. She doesn't explain it.
nodes.filePreview:
Background: Maren is in her late 30s. Worked for a decade as a licensed surveyor for the Admiralty before resigning under circumstances she doesn't discuss in detail. The official record says "voluntary withdrawal." People who were there say something on a mapping expedition went wrong and Maren came back alone. She hasn't filed an Admiralty survey since. She does freelance work now — private clients, academic commissions, the occasional job that pays well and asks few questions.

Current situation: She arrived in Tiveck three weeks ago, ostensibly to document the harbor's shifting sandbar pat…

nodes.name: MSBY Black Jackals
nodes.excerpt: MSBY Black Jackals is a Division 1 team in the V-League.

nodes.name: Natsu Hinata
nodes.excerpt: Natsu Hinata is Shōyō Hinata's younger sister.

nodes.name: Nekoma's
nodes.excerpt: Nekoma's is the coffeeshop where Bokuto, Kuroo, and Lev work.

nodes.name: Nishinoya
nodes.excerpt: Hinata and Kageyama sitting together, just behind Tanaka and Nishinoya.

nodes.name: Oikawa
nodes.excerpt: Oikawa wanted to hit something.
nodes.notes: Oikawa is a character who experiences frustration after losing a match to Karasuno. Despite his competitive nature, he acknowledges the fairness of the game. Oikawa is intrigued by the close relationship between Kageyama and Hinata, feeling a mix of envy and amusement. His interactions with Iwaizumi reveal a playful dynamic.
nodes.filePreview: Oikawa is a character who experiences frustration after losing a match to Karasuno. Despite his competitive nature, he acknowledges the fairness of the game. Oikawa is intrigued by the close relationship between Kageyama and Hinata, feeling a mix of envy and amusement. His interactions with Iwaizumi reveal a playful dynamic.

nodes.name: Orrus Vane
nodes.excerpt: A person with whom Fen Caldra has a complex, functional arrangement.
nodes.notes:
Background: Orrus is in his early 60s. Has been harbor master of Tiveck for nineteen years. Before that, two decades in the Admiralty's port administration division. He is meticulous, institutional, and deeply invested in appearing legitimate. His record, reviewed by anyone who looks at the official documents, is clean. The official documents are incomplete.

The irregularity: Sixteen years ago, Orrus accepted payment — substantial payment — to look away from a series of cargo movements through Tiveck's harbor over several years. He did not ask what the cargo was. He adjusted berthing records, reassigned inspection schedules, quietly archived documentation that should have been forwarded to the Admiralty. He believed, at the time, that the arrangement was manageable and temporary. It ran for six years.

The Warden's Seal: Fen Caldra holds a forged document that connects Orrus to one of those cargo movements by name. Orrus has seen it. He does not know how Fen obtained it or whether copies exist. He has been quietly cooperative with Fen's operations for eleven years as a result. He considers this preferable to the alternative. He has not, in that time, done anything that would give Fen additional leverage, which he considers a form of partial redemption.

Relationship with Maren Ashveil: He gave her access to harbor records because refusing would have looked suspicious, and because he calculated that the records she'd see are incomplete enough that she won't find what she's actually looking for. He may have miscalculated. He has been watching her progress with careful attention. He is not certain whether she is working for the Admiralty, for a private client, or for herself.

What he actually wants: For the last sixteen years to remain exactly where they are. He is four years from the standard retirement age. He has a pension. He has a house with a garden on the south end of Tiveck. He would like, very much, to get there without anything unraveling.

Personality: Formal in manner, precise in speech. Gives the impression of thoroughness. Has a way of answering questions completely while not quite answering what was asked. He is not cruel and not ambitious. He made one decision under pressure and has been managing its consequences ever since.

Physical details: Tall, slightly stooped. White hair kept short. Reads without spectacles but holds documents at arm's length. Always carries a pocket notebook filled with his own administrative shorthand.

nodes.name: Rella Corse
nodes.excerpt: Rella Corse spent eleven years as a Navigator First Class in the Admiralty before her dismissal.
nodes.notes:
Rella Corse is a former Navigator First Class in the Admiralty, dismissed under vague charges of 'conduct inconsistent with the duties of the navigational corps.' It is suggested that her dismissal was due to a formal complaint she filed against Harwick Ossel, accusing him of falsifying survey coordinates. After her dismissal, she worked as a freelance chart corrector for independent merchants and lived in Salt Warren. She is in her early forties, lean, and carries a brass sighting instrument. Rella is known for her formal demeanor and has a complex relationship with Dovan Strake.

Rella Corse is a former Navigator of the Admiralty who was quietly dismissed under sealed circumstances. She is a founding member of the Tide Compact, where she manages logistics using a storehouse at the edge of the 14th Lowland as cover. Known for her meticulous nature and quiet demeanor, Rella has a reputation for decisively cutting ties with those who jeopardize the operation. Her strategic use of legitimate contracts for salt-drying provides a facade for the Compact's activities.

Her strategic use of legitimate contracts for salt-drying provides a facade for the Compact's activities.

nodes.name: Sable Voss
nodes.excerpt: Sable Voss is Dellan Moor's companion and former supply administrator in the 14th Lowland regiment.
nodes.notes:
Background: Sable is 32. She spent six years as a supply and requisitions administrator in the 14th Lowland Regiment — the person responsible for tracking what the regiment had, what it needed, where it was supposed to be, and the considerable gap between those categories. She was good at the work. She was also good at understanding how institutional systems generate paperwork that nobody actually reads, and how to operate in that space. This is a skill set that transfers.

Why she deserted: The direct cause was an order to falsify supply records to obscure a shortage that would have been embarrassing to the regiment's commanding officer. This was not the first time. It was the first time she'd been directly ordered to do it in writing. She kept the order. She destroyed the falsified records. She was aware that neither of these choices was safe. She told Dellan what she'd done and that she was leaving. He asked if she wanted company.

Current situation: She and Dellan have been at the waystation for eight months. She manages most of their logistics — tracking their supplies, maintaining the rotation of preserved food, calculating how long they can sustain themselves without a market run. She has applied the same administrative competence to their survival that she formerly applied to the regiment's, and the results are considerably more honest.

What she's working on: She has been, slowly and carefully, drafting an account of the supply falsification she witnessed over six years — dates, quantities, names, the paper trail as she remembers it. She hasn't decided what to do with it. It is evidence of something worth investigating. It is also the kind of document that, if it surfaced, would make clear she existed. She keeps it separate from their other papers, in a sealed bundle under a flagstone in the back room.

Relationship with Dellan: She initiated what they have now, insofar as either of them initiated it. She is clearer than he is that it's real and that she wants it to be permanent. She hasn't said this directly because the situation makes permanence a complicated concept. She is waiting for a moment when it makes more sense to say it.

Personality: Organized, direct, drily funny in private. Less forthcoming with people she doesn't trust. Has a low tolerance for being managed or handled. Will tell you exactly what she thinks if you ask and sometimes if you don't.

Physical details: Medium height, pale, sharp-featured. Has a habit of counting things — supplies, paces, days. The counting is usually accurate. Keeps the sealed bundle and the order she kept in the same hiding place and checks it is still there approximately once a week.

nodes.name: Sable Voss's Account
nodes.excerpt: A draft account of supply falsification in the 14th Lowland Regiment.
nodes.notes: Sable Voss is drafting an account of the supply falsification she witnessed over six years, including dates, quantities, names, and the paper trail as she remembers it. She keeps it separate from other papers, in a sealed bundle under a flagstone.

nodes.name: Salt Warren
nodes.excerpt: A network of tidal caves and chambers used for gray-market storage.
nodes.notes:
What it is: A network of tidal caves and excavated chambers in the limestone cliff face on the eastern side of Tiveck's harbor. The natural caves were expanded and connected over many generations — originally by fishermen who needed cold storage, then by people with other storage requirements. The Warren has been a fixture of Tiveck's gray economy for at least two hundred years. It predates the current harbor master's office, the customs house, and every piece of Admiralty regulation governing the port.

Access: There are two ways in. The tidal passage is a low stone tunnel at the base of the cliff, navigable by small boat for approximately four hours either side of low tide. It's not secret — local fishermen know it exists — but the inner chambers require knowledge of which passages dead-end, which flood, and which lead somewhere useful. The interior entrance is a concealed door set into the cliff face, accessible from a storage yard behind a cooperage on the eastern quay. Fen Caldra holds the key. One other person has had it. That person is no longer in Tiveck.

Current use: Fen uses three of the inner chambers. One for bonded cargo awaiting quiet transfer. One for items he's holding on behalf of clients. One — deeper, drier, at the back of the network where the stone keeps a constant temperature — for things he hasn't decided what to do with. The Sunken Ledger is in this third chamber, in a sealed tin case behind a false section of wall that was built into the original excavation.

The space itself: Cold. Smells of brine and old stone. The chambers that have been in continuous use are equipped with lantern hooks, flat-topped stones used as table surfaces, and in one case a battered wooden chair that has been there for decades. The deeper chambers are raw limestone. Sound carries strangely. At high tide, you can hear the sea pushing against the outer wall.

What Maren suspects: She has identified the tidal passage in her surveying work and knows the eastern cliff contains substantial excavated space beyond what's described in official records. She hasn't been inside. She has asked Fen about it, indirectly. He gave her a partial answer. She is deciding whether to push further.

nodes.name: Shōyō Hinata
nodes.excerpt: Shōyō Hinata is the main protagonist of the Haikyū!! series.
nodes.notes:
Shōyō Hinata is a passionate and energetic volleyball player who serves as the main protagonist of the Haikyū!! series. Initially a first-year student at Karasuno High, he plays as a middle blocker for the Boys' Volleyball Team. Despite his short stature, Hinata is known for his incredible jumping ability and tenacity on the court. Inspired by the 'Small Giant,' a former ace of Karasuno, Hinata is determined to become a formidable player despite his height. Over the series, he evolves from a nervous and inexperienced player into a key member of his team, known for his quick attacks and ability to motivate those around him. His journey takes him from Karasuno High to playing for the MSBY Black Jackals in the V-League and eventually joining the Japan Men's National Volleyball Team during the 2021 Olympics. As of 2022, Hinata is a member of Asas São Paulo in the Brazil Super League.

Hinata is a lively and energetic character, often seen chattering and making gestures while eating. He has a close relationship with Kageyama, often seen interacting closely with him. Despite being scolded by Kageyama, Hinata seems to enjoy his company and reciprocates his gestures of care.

Shouyou is a young, energetic boy who looks up to his father, Bokuto, and shares his enthusiasm for volleyball. He is depicted as having a crush on his classmate Kageyama, despite Kageyama's often grumpy demeanor. Shouyou's interactions with Kageyama are a mix of admiration and frustration, as he struggles to win over Kageyama's friendship. His character is marked by a sense of determination and a desire to improve, both in his social interactions and in his volleyball skills.

nodes.name: Small Giant
nodes.excerpt: The Small Giant was a former ace of Karasuno High.

nodes.name: Sugawara
nodes.excerpt: Sugawara watched his sleeping kouhais with pride.
nodes.notes: Sugawara is portrayed as a nurturing figure, watching over his younger teammates with a sense of pride. He enjoys the tranquility of bus rides and feels a paternal connection to his teammates, often referred to as 'his kids.' Sugawara captures moments of camaraderie and affection among the team, such as taking a photo of Hinata and Kageyama sleeping together.

nodes.name: Sunken Ledger
nodes.excerpt: The Sunken Ledger is a valuable and dangerous artifact hidden by Fen Caldra.
nodes.notes: The Sunken Ledger is an artifact that Fen Caldra found six years ago in a sealed crate within the Salt Warren. He read enough of it to understand its value and danger. Fen has kept it hidden in a location only he knows, contemplating its fate for years without deciding.

nodes.name: Supply Falsification Order
nodes.excerpt: A written order to falsify supply records in the 14th Lowland Regiment.
nodes.notes: This order was given to Sable Voss to obscure a supply shortage that would have embarrassed the regiment's commanding officer. It was the first time she was directly ordered to falsify records in writing.

nodes.name: Tanaka
nodes.excerpt: Hinata and Kageyama sitting together, just behind Tanaka.

nodes.name: The Wrecked Compass
nodes.excerpt: A dockside tavern on the eastern quay known for its weathered compass rose.
nodes.notes: The Wrecked Compass is a dockside tavern located on the eastern quay, occupying the ground floor of a narrow stone building. It is named after a compass rose painted on its exterior wall, now weathered and illegible. The tavern is known for its low ceilings, warm atmosphere, and the smell of salt and woodsmoke. It serves primarily dock workers, small-cargo operators, and travelers who avoid larger taverns frequented by Admiralty personnel. Regulars are loyal and discreet, and new faces are noted. Fen Caldra is a frequent visitor, and the Deepwater Guild meets here informally. Maren Ashveil has visited twice to meet Fen. The tavern has a back room available for private hire.
nodes.filePreview:
What it is: A dockside tavern on the eastern quay, occupying the ground floor of a narrow stone building that predates most of its neighbors. Named for a compass rose painted on the exterior wall in a previous century, now so weathered that the directions are illegible. The interior is low-ceilinged, consistently warm, and smells of salt and woodsmoke. It has been continuously operated as a drinking establishment for longer than anyone can reliably document.

Who drinks here: Primarily dock workers from the eastern quay, small-cargo operators, and the kind of travelers who know to avoid the…

nodes.name: Tide Compact
nodes.excerpt: The Tide Compact is associated with The Inkhand.
nodes.notes: The Tide Compact is a smuggling consortium formed twelve years ago by three rival groups who agreed to cooperate for profit. Its founding members include Rella Corse, Dovan Strake, and the mysterious Inkhand. The Compact specializes in moving goods classified as contraband by the Admiralty, such as preserved meats, salvaged navigational instruments, and contested documents. The organization operates discreetly, with Rella Corse managing logistics and Dovan Strake handling buyers. The Inkhand's role remains ambiguous, with speculation about their connections to the Deepwater Guild.

The organization operates discreetly, with Rella Corse managing logistics and Dovan Strake handling buyers. The Inkhand's role remains ambiguous, with speculation about their connections to the Deepwater Guild.

nodes.name: Tiveck
nodes.excerpt: A harbor town with shifting sandbar patterns.
nodes.notes:
Overview: Tiveck is a mid-sized port city on the northern coast. Population somewhere between eight and twelve thousand depending on the season — the harbor draws transient workers, sailors, traders, and people who prefer cities where no one asks where you came from. The permanent residents are mostly families who have been here for generations, working the docks, the fisheries, the chandlers and sailmakers and ropewalks that service the maritime economy. The city has an Admiralty administrative presence but not a garrison — close enough to the capital's reach to follow the law most of the time, far enough to be casual about it.

The harbor: Deep-water berths on the western quay, shallow-draft moorings on the eastern side near the cliff face. The harbor master's office sits on a promontory above the western quay with a clear view of arrivals. The sandbar at the harbor mouth shifts seasonally and has been a navigation hazard for as long as anyone can remember — there's a licensed surveyor updating the charts every few years, which is why it didn't seem remarkable when Maren Ashveil showed up.

The eastern cliff: The cliff face that brackets the harbor's eastern edge is old limestone, honeycombed with natural caves that have been expanded, connected, and built into over centuries. Officially, the lower chambers are used for cold storage of perishables. Unofficially, the deeper chambers are the Salt Warren — accessible by a tidal passage that's navigable for about four hours around low tide, or through a concealed interior entrance that Fen Caldra knows and very few others do.

The administrative district: The harbor master's office, the customs house, and the Admiralty's local records depot are all clustered near the western quay. The records depot has not been properly staffed in years — there's a part-time archivist who comes in three days a week. This is relevant to what Maren found in the archives.

Atmosphere: Salt-weathered stone. Densely built near the waterfront, spreading into lower-density residential streets further inland. Always smells like the sea. Foggy in the mornings for much of the year. The kind of city where you can disappear easily if you want to or stay forever if you don't.

nodes.name: Tsukishima
nodes.excerpt: Tsukishima would rather not sit here.
nodes.notes: Tsukishima is a character who prefers to sit with people he knows rather than strangers, even if he finds them annoying. He observes the habits of his friends, Hinata and Kageyama, with a mix of amusement and disdain. Despite his sarcastic remarks, he seems to have a level of understanding and familiarity with them.

He observes the habits of his friends, Hinata and Kageyama, with a mix of amusement and disdain. Despite his sarcastic remarks, he seems to have a level of understanding and familiarity with them.

nodes.name: Virsa Col
nodes.excerpt: Virsa Col is a retired records archivist with a secretive past.
nodes.notes: Virsa Col, in her late 50s, worked as a part-time records archivist at the Admiralty's records depot in Tiveck for eleven years before retiring. She is known for her preference for quiet and meticulous record-keeping. Sixteen years ago, she misfiled cargo inspection reports at the request of Orris Vane, which she has since regretted. Maren Ashveil is close to discovering her involvement. Virsa lives alone, enjoys gardening, and reads extensively. She is quiet, careful, and has a strong sense of morality, though she struggles with past decisions.
nodes.filePreview:
Background: Virsa is in her late 50s. She worked as a part-time records archivist at the Admiralty's records depot in Tiveck for eleven years before retiring. The depot's incomplete staffing — three days a week, never fully resourced — suited her preference for uninterrupted quiet. She knows the archive's gaps better than anyone, because she was the one who created them. Not through malice. Through omission.

The irregularity: Sixteen years ago, she accepted a small sum to misfile a series of incoming cargo inspection reports — not destroy them, just reclassify them under an obscure administ…

nodes.name: Warden's Seal
nodes.excerpt: A forged customs clearance document with strategic leverage.
nodes.notes:
What it is: A forged customs clearance document — single folio, official Admiralty format, bearing what appears to be the counter-seal of the Port Warden's office. The document certifies the inspection and clearance of a specific cargo shipment that was in fact never inspected. It bears Orrus Vane's administrative identifier as the responsible officer, inserted there by whoever commissioned the forgery.

Origin: Fen Caldra had it made approximately twelve years ago, during a period when he was consolidating his position in Tiveck's gray market and wanted insurance against the people he was working with. He used a document forger who has since left Tiveck. The quality of the forgery is high — it would hold up to most scrutiny, though an expert examiner with access to the original counter-seal records could identify it.

Why Fen has it: He has never used it as leverage in any active sense. He has not threatened Orrus with it or made explicit demands. He simply, on one occasion years ago, made sure Orrus knew he had it. The knowledge has been sufficient. Orrus has been carefully cooperative with Fen's harbor operations ever since — nothing dramatic, just a consistent absence of complications.

Where it's kept: Fen does not keep it in the Salt Warren. He keeps it in a sealed envelope in a strongbox at a lodging house in the inland district of Tiveck, under a name that is not his. He pays the storage fee a year at a time. He has done this for twelve years.

Strategic value: The document ties Orrus to a specific shipment. The shipment, if investigated, connects to other things. Fen has never followed that chain all the way — he has enough of an idea to understand the danger and has chosen not to know more. Maren, with her Admiralty background, would likely recognize the significance of the specific cargo designation on the document if she ever saw it.

What a resolution might look like: The Seal and the Sunken Ledger are both leverage artifacts pointing at the same network. In combination they would be harder to dismiss than either alone. Whether that combination ever happens depends on what Maren and Fen eventually decide to tell each other.

nodes.name: Waystation on the Moorland
nodes.excerpt: A disused highwayman's waystation where Dellan Moor and Sable Voss live.
nodes.notes: The waystation on the moorland is a disused highwayman's station located north of the main coastal road. It is approximately two days' walk from the nearest town of any size. Dellan Moor and Sable Voss have been living there for eight months, utilizing its mostly intact roof, fireplace, and working well. They have stopped moving because the waystation is sufficient for their needs.

nodes.name: Yamaguchi
nodes.excerpt: Even Yamaguchi thought the same.

links.label: knows concealed entrance to Salt Warren
